entries.d: To plan and set up something
entries.s: Let's _____ the party and plan who to invite and what food to buy.

entries.d: A party that someone does not know about before
entries.s: We planned a _____ for my sister. She does not know about it yet!

entries.d: The money you pay to go into a place
entries.s: The _____ for the zoo is five euros — you pay it to get in.

entries.d: Someone who makes pictures, music, or other creative things
entries.s: This painting is beautiful. The _____ is very talented.

entries.d: A place where people show art or other things
entries.s: We went to see a photo _____ at the town hall.

entries.d: Not clean
entries.s: My shoes are very _____ because I played in the mud.

entries.d: New and from the time we live in now
entries.s: Their school is very _____ with big windows and computers.

entries.d: A building where you can see old or interesting things
entries.s: We saw old coins and swords at the _____.

entries.d: To belong to something bigger
entries.s: This song is _____ the new album — it is one of the twelve tracks on it.

entries.d: Art made from stone, wood, or metal that you can walk around
entries.s: There is a big stone _____ of a horse that an artist made for the park last year.

entries.d: To have a price or value
entries.s: This old painting is _____ a lot of money.

entries.d: A question you ask when someone looks sad or worried
entries.s: You look sad. _____? Tell me what is wrong.

entries.d: Any person at all
entries.s: Is _____ at home? I knocked but nobody opened.

entries.d: How a person acts and what they do
entries.s: Good _____ in class makes the teacher happy — sitting still, listening, and not talking.

entries.d: To call, write, or send a message to someone
entries.s: Please _____ me by phone or email if you need help.

entries.d: When things are not tidy and everywhere
entries.s: My room is a big _____ with clothes on the floor and toys everywhere. I need to clean it.

entries.d: To give something to another person
entries.s: Can you _____ this letter to your mum? I cannot see her today.

entries.d: A secret word you use to open your account
entries.s: Never tell your _____ to other people — it is secret and protects your account.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with PASSWORD?

entries.d: To put a message or picture on the internet
entries.s: She _____ a funny photo on Instagram for all her followers to see, and got many likes.

entries.d: A message or picture on the internet
entries.s: Did you read his new _____ on social media about his holiday photos?

entries.d: a word used before a/an + adjective + noun to show emphasis
entries.s: It was _____ a nice day -- so warm and sunny -- that we went to the lake.

entries.d: A helpful idea or piece of advice
entries.s: Here is a good _____ that will help you pass the test: read the questions first before you answer.

entries.d: Something that can happen or be done
entries.s: Is it _____ to finish this homework in ten minutes?

entries.d: Really great and amazing
entries.s: The fireworks last night were _____!

entries.d: Not interesting at all
entries.s: This TV show is so _____. Nothing interesting happens and I want to fall asleep.

entries.d: Hard to understand
entries.s: The instructions were very _____ and I made mistakes. Nothing made sense.

entries.d: Not easy to do or understand
entries.s: The last question on the test was really _____. Nobody in the class could answer it.

entries.d: Making you feel happy and full of energy
entries.s: Going on a roller coaster is really _____!

entries.d: Something that makes you laugh
entries.s: The clown at the party was very _____. He made everyone laugh out loud.

entries.d: Feeling bad because you did something silly
entries.s: I felt so _____ when I fell in front of everyone — my face turned red and I wanted to hide.

entries.d: A flat round thing you put food on
entries.s: Put the pasta on the _____ and start eating.

entries.d: Something you do not tell other people
entries.s: Can you keep a _____? Don't tell anyone — not even your best friend!

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with SECRET?

entries.d: Feeling sad or angry about something bad
entries.s: She was _____ because someone took her pencil case. Tears came to her eyes.

entries.d: To put something more to what is already there
entries.s: You can _____ sugar to your tea if you want to make it sweeter.

entries.d: To not pass a test or not do well enough
entries.s: I studied hard every night because I did not want to _____ the exam and get a bad mark.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with FAIL?

entries.d: Words you say to show you will do something for sure
entries.s: I will help you tomorrow. _____ I always keep my word.
